text: How many squares of ALL sizes (1×1, 2×2, ... 8×8) can be found on a standard 8×8 chessboard?
You have exactly 6 matchsticks of equal length. What is the maximum number of equilateral triangles you can form (each stick = one full side)?
explanation: In 2D: max 2 triangles. In 3D: form a tetrahedron (triangular pyramid) — it has 4 equilateral faces and exactly 6 edges. Answer: 4.

text: What is the minimum number of straight cuts to divide a circular pizza into exactly 7 pieces? (Pieces need NOT be equal.)
explanation: Max regions with n cuts: R(n) = n(n+1)/2 + 1. With 3 cuts: 3(4)/2+1 = 7 ✓. Key: each new cut must cross all previous cuts inside the circle.
